description: Need a Makefile at /home/user/docs/Makefile that builds our markdown docs into html. Source files are in src/, output goes to build/. Just need a rule so `make html` converts every .md in src/ to a .html in build/ using pandoc. Nothing fancy.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/docs/ exists and is writable.
- /home/user/docs/src/ contains three markdown files: intro.md, guide.md, reference.md. Each has valid markdown content (headers, paragraphs, a list).
- /home/user/docs/build/ exists but is empty.
- /home/user/docs/Makefile does not exist.
- pandoc is installed and available in PATH.
- make (GNU Make) is installed.

Expected final state:
- /home/user/docs/Makefile exists.
- Running `cd /home/user/docs && make html` exits 0.
- After running make html, /home/user/docs/build/ contains intro.html, guide.html, and reference.html.
- Each .html file is valid pandoc output (contains `<html` or `<!DOCTYPE` or at minimum the converted content wrapped in html tags).
- Running `make html` a second time with no source changes either does nothing or succeeds without error (idempotent).

Invariants:
- /home/user/docs/src/*.md files unchanged.
- No files created outside /home/user/docs/.

Anti-shortcut guards:
- The Makefile must contain a pattern or rule that references pandoc — verified by `grep -i pandoc /home/user/docs/Makefile` returning a match.
- The Makefile must have a target named `html` — verified by `grep -E '^html[ ]*:' /home/user/docs/Makefile` or make reporting the target exists.
name: task_000000_9c7052a3
